info.title: e-conomic REST API
info.description: OpenAPI 3 description of the e-conomic REST API, generated from e-conomic's published per-endpoint JSON schemas. See https://restdocs.e-conomic.com/.

get.description: A schema for retrieval of a collection of accounting years.

200.description: A schema for retrieval of a collection of accounting years.

schema.description: A schema for retrieval of a collection of accounting years.

collection.description: The array containing all the accounting years on the agreement.

items.description: A specific accounting year on the agreement.

fromDate.description: The first date in the accounting year in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Except for the first accounting year on an agreement, it must be the date immediately following the previous accounting year, and thus must be the first day of a month. The first accounting year on an agreement can begin on any day of the month.

toDate.description: The last date in the accounting year in the format YYYY-MM-DD. It must be the last date in the last month of the accounting year. An accounting year can at most have a duration of 18 months.

closed.description: Determines if the accounting year is closed for further transactions.

year.description: The calendar year or years spanned by the accounting year in the format YYYY or YYYY/YYYY.

periods.description: A link to the collection of accounting year periods on an agreement.

entries.description: A link to a collection of all entries booked in the accounting year.

totals.description: A link to the chart of accounts with the years total in base currency.

vouchers.description: A link to a collection of vouchers created in the accounting year.

self.description: A unique link reference to the accounting year item.

metaData.description: Information about possible actions, endpoints and resource paths related to the endpoint.

self.description: The unique self reference of the accounting years collection.

post.description: A schema for creating an accounting year.

schema.description: A schema for creating an accounting year.

Except for the first accounting year on an agreement, it must be the date immediately following the previous accounting year, and thus must be the first day of a month. The first accounting year on an agreement can begin on any day of the month.

toDate.description: The last date in the accounting year in the format YYYY-MM-DD. It must be the last date in the last month of the accounting year. An accounting year can at most have a duration of 18 months.

200.description: A schema for creating an accounting year.

schema.description: A schema for creating an accounting year.

toDate.description: The last date in the accounting year in the format YYYY-MM-DD. It must be the last date in the last month of the accounting year. An accounting year can at most have a duration of 18 months.

get.description: A schema for retrieval of an accounting years.

200.description: A schema for retrieval of an accounting years.

schema.description: A schema for retrieval of an accounting years.

fromDate.description: The first date in the accounting year in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Except for the first accounting year on an agreement, it must be the date immediately following the previous accounting year, and thus must be the first day of a month. The first accounting year on an agreement can begin on any day of the month.

toDate.description: The last date in the accounting year in the format YYYY-MM-DD. It must be the last date in the last month of the accounting year. An accounting year can at most have a duration of 18 months.

closed.description: If true this indicates that the accounting year is closed for further transactions.

year.description: The calendar year or years spanned by the accounting year in the format YYYY or YYYY/YYYY.

periods.description: A link to the collection of accounting year periods in the accounting year.

entries.description: A link to a collection of all entries booked in the accounting year.

totals.description: A link to the chart of accounts with the years total in base currency.

vouchers.description: A link to a collection of vouchers created in the accounting year. This requires that international ledger is enabled.

self.description: A unique link reference to the accounting year item.

get.description: A schema for retrieving the entries for a specific accounting years.

200.description: A schema for retrieving the entries for a specific accounting years.

schema.description: A schema for retrieving the entries for a specific accounting years.

items.description: A specific entry in the accounting year.

account.description: The account the entry is connected to.

accountNumber.description: A unique identifier of the account.

self.description: A unique reference to the account resource.

supplierInvoiceNumber.description: A unique identifier of the supplier invoice.

amountInBaseCurrency.description: The total entry amount in base currency.

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the entry.

date.description: Entry issue date. The date is formatted according to ISO-8601(YYYY-MM-DD).

dueDate.description: The date the invoice is due for payment. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

departmentalDistribution.description: A departmental distribution defines which departments this entry is distributed between. This requires the departments module to be enabled.

departmentalDistributionNumber.description: A unique identifier of the departmental distribution.

self.description: A unique reference to the departmental distribution resource.

project.description: A reference to any project this entry might be related to. This requires the projects module to be enabled.

projectNumber.description: A unique identifier of the project.

self.description: A unique reference to the project resource.

costType.description: The cost type for this entity.

costTypeNumber.description: The unique identifier of cost type.

self.description: A unique link reference to the cost type.

entryNumber.description: The unique identifier of the entry line.

text.description: A short description about the entry.

entryType.description: The type of entry.

vatCode.description: The unique identifier of the vat account.

self.description: A unique link reference to the vatAccount item.

customer.description: The customer for this entity.

customerNumber.description: The unique identifier of customer.

self.description: A unique link reference to the customer.

supplierNumber.description: The supplier number is a positive unique numerical identifier with a maximum of 9 digits.

supplierGroup.description: The supplier group for this supplier.

supplierGroupNumber.description: The supplier group number is a positive unique numerical identifier with a maximum of 9 digits.

self.description: A unique link reference to the supplier group.
A unique link reference to the supplier.

unitNumber.description: The unique identifier of the unit.

unit2.description: The second unit of measure applied to the entry. Requires dimension module.

self.description: A unique reference to the booked invoice resource.

invoiceNumber.description: Unique identifier for reminders and invoices.

remainderInBaseCurrency.description: The remainder in base currency on the entry.

paymentDetails.description: Payment details associated with the supplier invoice. Each payment detail contains two keys that are required. For the +71 type, fiSupplierNo and ocrLine is expected. For the +73 type, fiSupplierNo and message is expected. For the +04 type, giroAccount and ocrLine is expected. For the bank transfer type, accountNo and message is expected. For the IBAN type, ibanSwift and message is expected. For the +75 type, fiSupplierNo and ocrLineMessage is expected. Please refer to the schema for validation rules details.

paymentType.description: A specific payment type on the entry.

paymentTypeNumber.description: The payment type number is a positive unique numerical identifier.

self.description: A unique link reference to the payment type item.
A unique reference to the entry resource.

balance.description: The current balanace of the account.

draftBalance.description: The current balance of the account including draft (not yet booked) entries.

barred.description: Shows if the account is barred from being used.

name.description: The name of the department.

barred.description: Indicates if the department is barred from usage. Default is false.

departmentalDistribution.description: Information about a departmental distribution.

departmentalDistributionNumber.description: A unique numeric identifier for the departmental distribution.

name.description: A descriptive name of the specific departmental distribution.

distributionType.description: This property indicates whether the distribution is system generated from a departmentor if it is one created by a user. In order to make it easy to distribute everything to one department, the system generates a distribution for each department.

distributions.description: A list of departments and their share of the distribution in percentage. The percentages always add up to 100%.

items.description: Description of the distributions that make up a departmental distribution.

percentage.description: The percentage distributed to this department.

departmentNumber.description: A unique identifier of the department.

self.description: The unique self reference of the department.

remainderInBaseCurrency.description: The remainder in base currency on the entry.

paymentDetails.description: Payment details associated with the supplier invoice. Each payment detail contains two keys that are required. For the +71 type, fiSupplierNo and ocrLine is expected. For the +73 type, fiSupplierNo and message is expected. For the +04 type, giroAccount and ocrLine is expected. For the bank transfer type, accountNo and message is expected. For the IBAN type, ibanSwift and message is expected. For the +75 type, fiSupplierNo and ocrLineMessage is expected. Please refer to the schema for validation rules details.

get.summary: Customer booked invoices collection GET schema
get.description: A schema for fetching a collection of booked invoices for a customer.

200.description: A schema for fetching a collection of booked invoices for a customer.

bookedInvoiceNumber.description: A reference number for the booked invoice document.

date.description: Invoice issue date. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the invoice.

netAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the invoice in the invoice currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the invoice in base currency.

remainderInBaseCurrency.description: Remaining amount to be paid in base currency.

dueDate.description: The date the invoice is due for payment. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). This is only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate'.

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the invoice.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the invoice. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type 'duedate

name.description: The name of the payment terms.

For instance, the customer placing the order may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the invoice may be ACME IT.

address.description: The street address of the actual recipient.

get.summary: Customer draft invoices collection GET schema
get.description: A schema for fetching a collection of draft invoices for a customer.

200.description: A schema for fetching a collection of draft invoices for a customer.

schema.description: A schema for fetching a collection of draft invoices for a customer.

draftInvoiceNumber.description: A reference number for the draft invoice document. This is not the final invoice number. The final invoice number can't be determined until the invoice is booked.

date.description: Invoice issue date. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the invoice.

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate between the invoice currency and the base currency of the agreement. The exchange rate expresses how much it will cost in base currency to buy 100 units of the invoice currency.

netAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the cost price of the items on the invoice and the sales net invoice amount in base currency. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

marginPercentage.description: The margin expressed as a percentage. If the net invoice amount is less than the cost price this number will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the invoice in the invoice currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the invoice in base currency.

costPriceInBaseCurrency.description: The total cost of the items on the invoice in the base currency of the agreement.

dueDate.description: The date the invoice is due for payment. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). This is only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate'.

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the invoice.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

customerNumber.description: The customer number is a positive unique numerical identifier with a maximum of 9 digits.

ean.description: European Article Number. EAN is used for invoicing the Danish public sector.

email.description: Customer e-mail address where e-conomic invoices should be emailed. Note: you can specify multiple email addresses in this field, separated by a space. If you need to send a copy of the invoice or write to other e-mail addresses, you can also create one or more customer contacts.

lastUpdated.description: The date this customer was last updated. The date is formatted according to ISO-8601.

name.description: The customer name.

publicEntryNumber.description: The public entry number is used for electronic invoicing, to define the account invoices will be registered on at the customer.

get.description: A schema for retrieving available endpoints of invoices.

200.description: A schema for retrieving available endpoints of invoices.

schema.description: A schema for retrieving available endpoints of invoices.

drafts.description: A reference to all unbooked invoices.

booked.description: A reference to all booked invoices.

paid.description: A reference to all paid invoices. Paid invoices are invoices that has no remaining unpaid amount.

unpaid.description: A reference to all unpaid invoices. unpaid invoices are invoices that has a remaining unpaid amount

overdue.description: A reference to all overdue invoices. Overdue invoices are unpaid invoices where due date has been surpassed.

notDue.description: A reference to all not due invoices. Not due invoices are invoices where due date still hasn't been surpassed. This includes invoices that are due today.

sent.description: A reference to all sent invoices.

totals.description: A reference to the totals of all invoices.

get.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of booked invoices.

200.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of booked invoices.

schema.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of booked invoices.

bookedInvoiceNumber.description: A reference number for the booked invoice document.

date.description: Invoice issue date. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the invoice.

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate between the invoice currency and the base currency of the agreement. The exchange rate expresses how much it will cost in base currency to buy 100 units of the invoice currency.

netAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the invoice in the invoice currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the invoice in base currency.

remainderInBaseCurrency.description: Remaining amount to be paid in base currency.

dueDate.description: The date the invoice is due for payment. Only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate', in which case it is mandatory. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the invoice.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the invoice. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type 'duedate

name.description: The name of the payment terms.

self.description: A unique reference to the payment term resource.

customer.description: The customer being invoiced.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.

recipient.description: The actual recipient of the invoice. This may be the same info found on the customer (and will probably be so in most cases) but it may also be a different recipient. For instance, the customer placing the order may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the invoice may be ACME IT.

name.description: The name of the actual recipient.

address.description: The street address of the actual recipient.

city.description: The city of the actual recipient.

country.description: The country of the actual recipient.

ean.description: The 'European Article Number' of the actual recipient.

publicEntryNumber.description: The public entry number of the actual recipient.

attention.description: The person to whom this invoice is addressed.

vatZoneNumber.description: Unique identifier of the vat zone.

get.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of draft invoices.

200.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of draft invoices.

schema.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of draft invoices.

draftInvoiceNumber.description: A reference number for the draft invoice document. This is not the final invoice number. The final invoice number can't be determined until the invoice is booked.

date.description: Invoice issue date. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the invoice.

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate between the invoice currency and the base currency of the agreement. The exchange rate expresses how much it will cost in base currency to buy 100 units of the invoice currency.

netAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the cost price of the items on the invoice and the sales net invoice amount in base currency. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

marginPercentage.description: The margin expressed as a percentage. If the net invoice amount is less than the cost price this number will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the invoice in the invoice currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the invoice in base currency.

costPriceInBaseCurrency.description: The total cost of the items on the invoice in the base currency of the agreement.

dueDate.description: The date the invoice is due for payment. Only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate', in which case it is mandatory. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the invoice.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the invoice. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type 'duedate

name.description: The name of the payment terms.

self.description: A unique reference to the payment term resource.

customer.description: The customer being invoiced.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

recipient.description: The actual recipient of the invoice. This may be the same info found on the customer (and will probably be so in most cases) but it may also be a different recipient. For instance, the customer placing the order may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the invoice may be ACME IT.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmount.description: The total invoice amount in the invoice currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

grossAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total invoice amount in the base currency of the agreement after all taxes and discounts have been applied. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the cost price of the items on the invoice and the sales net invoice amount in base currency. For a credit note this amount will be negative.

marginPercentage.description: The margin expressed as a percentage. If the net invoice amount is less than the cost price this number will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the invoice in the invoice currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the invoice in base currency.

costPriceInBaseCurrency.description: The total cost of the items on the invoice in the base currency of the agreement.

dueDate.description: The date the invoice is due for payment. Only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate', in which case it is mandatory. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the invoice.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the invoice. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type 'duedate

name.description: The name of the payment terms.

self.description: A unique reference to the payment term resource.

customer.description: The customer being invoiced.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.

recipient.description: The actual recipient of the invoice. This may be the same info found on the customer (and will probably be so in most cases) but it may also be a different recipient. For instance, the customer placing the order may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the invoice may be ACME IT.

name.description: The name of the actual recipient.

address.description: The street address of the actual recipient.

zip.description: The zip code of the actual recipient.

city.description: The city of the actual recipient.

country.description: The country of the actual recipient.

ean.description: The 'European Article Number' of the actual recipient.

publicEntryNumber.description: The public entry number of the actual recipient.

attention.description: The person to whom this invoice is addressed.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer employee.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer employee.

vatZoneNumber.description: Unique identifier of the vat zone.

self.description: A unique reference to the vat zone.

nemHandelType.description: Chosen NemHandel type used for e-invoicing.

deliveryLocation.description: A reference to the place of delivery for the goods on the invoice

deliveryLocationNumber.description: A unique identifier for the delivery location.

self.description: A unique reference to the delivery location resource.

delivery.description: The actual place of delivery for the goods on the invoice. This is usually the same place as the one referenced in the deliveryLocation property, but may be edited as required.

address.description: Street address where the goods must be delivered to the customer.

zip.description: The zip code of the place of delivery.

city.description: The city of the place of delivery

country.description: The country of the place of delivery

deliveryTerms.description: Details about the terms of delivery.

heading.description: The invoice heading. Usually displayed at the top of the invoice.

textLine1.description: The first line of supplementary text on the invoice. This is usually displayed right under the heading in a smaller font.

textLine2.description: The second line of supplementary text in the notes on the invoice. This is usually displayed as a footer on the invoice.

references.description: Customer and company references related to this invoice.

customerContact.description: The customer contact is a reference to the employee at the customer to contact regarding the invoice.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer contact.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer contact resource.

salesPerson.description: The sales person is a reference to the employee who sold the goods on the invoice. This is also the person who is credited with this sale in reports.

vendorReference.description: A reference to any second employee involved in the sale.

self.description: A unique reference to the employee resource.

other.description: A text field that can be used to save any custom reference on the invoice.

project.description: The project the invoice is connected to.

projectNumber.description: A unique identifier of the project.

paymentDetails.description: Payment details associated with the supplier invoice. Each payment detail contains two keys that are required. For the +71 type, fiSupplierNo and ocrLine is expected. For the +73 type, fiSupplierNo and message is expected. For the +04 type, giroAccount and ocrLine is expected. For the bank transfer type, accountNo and message is expected. paymentDetails.description: Payment details associated with the supplier invoice. Each payment detail contains two keys that are required. For the +71 type, fiSupplierNo and ocrLine is expected. For the +73 type, fiSupplierNo and message is expected. For the +04 type, giroAccount and ocrLine is expected. For the bank transfer type, accountNo and message is expected. netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total order amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the order in the order currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the order in base currency.

costPriceInBaseCurrency.description: The total cost of the items on the order in the base currency of the agreement.

The unique self reference of the specific draft order.

metaData.description: Information about possible actions, endpoints and resource paths related to the endpoint.

self.description: The unique self reference of the draft order collection.

post.description: A schema for creating a draft order.

schema.description: A schema for creating a draft order.

date.description: Order issue date. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

currency.description: The ISO 4217 3-letter currency code of the order.

exchangeRate.description: The desired exchange rate between the order currency and the base currency of the agreement. The exchange rate expresses how much it will cost in base currency to buy 100 units of the order currency. If no exchange rate is supplied, the system will get the current daily rate, unless the order currency is the same as the base currency, in which case it will be set to 100.

dueDate.description: The date the order is due for payment. This property is only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate', in which case it is a mandatory property. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

grossAmount.description: The total order amount in the order currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the cost price of the items on the order and the sales net order amount in base currency.

marginPercentage.description: The margin expressed as a percentage. If the net order amount is less than the cost price this number will be negative.

netAmount.description: The total order amount in the order currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the order in base currency.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the order in the order currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

layout.description: The layout used by the order.

layoutNumber.description: A unique identifier of the layout.

self.description: A unique reference to the layout resource.

project.description: The project the order is connected to.

projectNumber.description: A unique identifier of the project.

self.description: A unique reference to the project resource.

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the order.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the order. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type 'duedate

self.description: A unique reference to the payment term resource.

customer.description: The customer of the order.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.

recipient.description: The actual recipient of the order. This may be the same info found on the customer (and will probably be so in most cases) but it may also be a different recipient. For instance, the customer placing the order may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the order may be ACME IT.

name.description: The name of the actual recipient.

address.description: The street address of the actual recipient.

zip.description: The zip code of the actual recipient.

city.description: The city of the actual recipient.

country.description: The country of the actual recipient.

ean.description: The 'European Article Number' of the actual recipient.

publicEntryNumber.description: The public entry number of the actual recipient.

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate between the order currency and the base currency of the agreement. The exchange rate expresses how much it will cost in base currency to buy 100 units of the order currency.

netAmount.description: The total order amount in the order currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total order amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

grossAmount.description: The total order amount in the order currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

grossAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total order amount in the base currency of the agreement after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the cost price of the items on the order and the sales net order amount in base currency.

marginPercentage.description: The margin expressed as a percentage. If the net order amount is less than the cost price this number will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the order in the order currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the order in base currency.

costPriceInBaseCurrency.description: The total cost of the items on the order in the base currency of the agreement.

dueDate.description: The date the order, is due for payment. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). This is only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate'.

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the order.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the order. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type duedate.

name.description: The name of the payment terms.

self.description: A unique reference to the payment term resource.

customer.description: The customer of the order.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.

recipient.description: The actual recipient of the order. This may be the same info found on the customer (and will probably be so in most cases) but it may also be a different recipient. For instance, the customer placing the order may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the order may be ACME IT.

name.description: The name of the actual recipient.

address.description: The street address of the actual recipient.

zip.description: The zip code of the actual recipient.

city.description: The city of the actual recipient.

country.description: The country of the actual recipient.

ean.description: The 'European Article Number' of the actual recipient.

publicEntryNumber.description: The public entry number of the actual recipient.

attention.description: The person to whom this order is addressed.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer employee.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer employee.

vatZoneNumber.description: Unique identifier of the vat zone.

self.description: A unique reference to the vat zone.

nemHandelType.description: Chosen NemHandel type used for e-invoicing.

deliveryLocation.description: A reference to the place of delivery for the goods on the order

deliveryLocationNumber.description: A unique identifier for the delivery location.

self.description: A unique reference to the delivery location resource.

delivery.description: The actual place of delivery for the goods on the order. This is usually the same place as the one referenced in the deliveryLocation property, but may be edited as required.

address.description: Street address where the goods must be delivered to the customer.

zip.description: The zip code of the place of delivery.

city.description: The city of the place of delivery

country.description: The country of the place of delivery

deliveryTerms.description: Details about the terms of delivery.

heading.description: The order heading. Usually displayed at the top of the order.

textLine1.description: The first line of supplementary text on the order. This is usually displayed right under the heading in a smaller font.

textLine2.description: The second line of supplementary text in the notes on the order. This is usually displayed as a footer on the order.

references.description: Customer and company references related to this order.

customerContact.description: The customer contact is a reference to the employee at the customer to contact regarding the order.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer contact.

customer.description: The customer this contact belongs to.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.

salesPerson.description: The sales person is a reference to the employee who sold the goods on the order. This is also the person who is credited with this sale in reports.

vendorReference.description: A reference to any second employee involved in the sale.

self.description: A unique reference to the employee resource.

other.description: A text field that can be used to save any custom reference on the order.

project.description: The project the order is connected to.

projectNumber.description: A unique identifier of the project.

pdf.description: References a pdf representation of this order.

download.description: The unique reference of the pdf representation for this order.

orderHandle.description: The unique reference of the SOAP order handle.

id.description: ID of the SOAP order handle.

templates.description: References to all templates on this order.

lines.description: An array containing the specific order lines.

items.description: An array of the order lines that make up the order.

lineNumber.description: The line number is a unique number within the order.

sortKey.description: A sort key used to sort the lines in ascending order within the order.

description.description: A description of the product or service sold.

accrual.description: Accrual is used to allocate costs and/or revenues over several periods.

startDate.description: The start date for the accrual. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

endDate.description: The end date for the accrual. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

unit.description: The unit of measure applied to the order line.

unitNumber.description: The unique identifier of the unit.

name.description: The name of the unit (e.g. 'kg' for weight or 'l' for volume).

product.description: The product or service offered on the order line.

quantity.description: The number of units of goods on the order line.

unitNetPrice.description: The price of 1 unit of the goods or services on the order line in the order currency.

discountPercentage.description: A line discount expressed as a percentage.

unitCostPrice.description: The cost price of 1 unit of the goods or services in the order currency.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the net price and the cost price on the order line in base currency.

marginPercentage.description: The margin on the order line expressed as a percentage.

get.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of archived quotes.

200.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of archived quotes.

schema.description: A schema for retrieving a collection of archived quotes.

quoteNumber.description: A reference number for the quote document.

date.description: quote issue date. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the quote.

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate between the quote currency and the base currency of the agreement. The exchange rate expresses how much it will cost in base currency to buy 100 units of the quote currency.

netAmount.description: The total quote amount in the quote currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total quote amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

grossAmount.description: The total quote amount in the quote currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

grossAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total quote amount in the base currency of the agreement after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the cost price of the items on the quote and the sales net quote amount in base currency.

marginPercentage.description: The margin expressed as a percentage. If the net quote amount is less than the cost price this number will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the quote in the quote currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the quote in base currency.

costPriceInBaseCurrency.description: The total cost of the items on the quote in the base currency of the agreement.

dueDate.description: The date the quote is due for payment. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). This is only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate'.

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the quote.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the quote. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type 'duedate

name.description: The name of the payment terms.

self.description: A unique reference to the payment term resource.

customer.description: The customer being quoted.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

recipient.description: The actual recipient of the quote. This may be the same info found on the customer (and will probably be so in most cases) but it may also be a different recipient. For instance, the customer placing the quote may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the quote may be ACME IT.

address.description: The street address of the actual recipient.

zip.description: The zip code of the actual recipient.

city.description: The city of the actual recipient.

country.description: The country of the actual recipient.

ean.description: The 'European Article Number' of the actual recipient.

publicEntryNumber.description: The public entry number of the actual recipient.

deliveryLocationNumber.description: A unique identifier for the delivery location.

self.description: A unique reference to the delivery location resource.

delivery.description: The actual place of delivery for the goods on the quote. This is usually the same place as the one referenced in the deliveryLocation property, but may be edited as required.

address.description: Street address where the goods must be delivered to the customer.

zip.description: The zip code of the place of delivery.

city.description: The city of the place of delivery

country.description: The country of the place of delivery

deliveryTerms.description: Details about the terms of delivery.

heading.description: The quote heading. Usually displayed at the top of the quote.

textLine1.description: The first line of supplementary text on the quote. This is usually displayed right under the heading in a smaller font.

textLine2.description: The second line of supplementary text in the notes on the quote. This is usually displayed as a footer on the quote.

references.description: Customer and company references related to this quote.

customerContact.description: The customer contact is a reference to the employee at the customer to contact regarding the quote.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer contact.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer contact resource.

salesPerson.description: The primary sales person is a reference to the employee who sold the goods on the quote.

self.description: A unique reference to the employee resource.

vendorReference.description: A reference to any second employee involved in the sale.

self.description: A unique reference to the employee resource.

other.description: A text field that can be used to save any custom reference on the quote.

pdf.description: References a pdf representation of this quote.

download.description: The unique reference of the pdf representation for this draft quote.

lines.description: An array containing the specific quote lines.

items.description: An array of the quote lines that make up the quote.

lineNumber.description: The line number is a unique number within the quote.

sortKey.description: A sort key used to sort the lines in ascending quote within the quote.

description.description: A description of the product or service sold. Please note, that when setting existing products, description field is required. While setting non-existing product, description field can remain empty.

accrual.description: The accrual for the quote.

startDate.description: The start date for the accrual. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

endDate.description: The end date for the accrual. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

unit.description: The unit of measure applied to the quote line.

unitNumber.description: The unique identifier of the unit.

self.description: A unique reference to the unit resource.

product.description: The product or service offered on the quote line.

productNumber.description: The unique product number. This can be a stock keeping unit identifier (SKU).

self.description: A unique reference to the product resource.

quantity.description: The number of units of goods on the quote line.

unitNetPrice.description: The price of 1 unit of the goods or services on the quote line in the quote currency.

discountPercentage.description: A line discount expressed as a percentage.

unitCostPrice.description: The cost price of 1 unit of the goods or services in the quote currency.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the net price and the cost price on the quote line in base currency.

marginPercentage.description: The margin on the quote line expressed as a percentage.

departmentalDistribution.description: A departmental distribution defines which departments this entry is distributed between. This requires the departments module to be enabled.

departmentalDistributionNumber.description: A unique identifier of the departmental distribution.

self.description: A unique reference to the departmental distribution resource.

get.description: A schema for retrieving a draft quote.

200.description: A schema for retrieving a draft quote.

schema.description: A schema for retrieving a draft quote.

quoteNumber.description: A reference number for the quote document.

date.description: quote issue date. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the quote.

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate between the quote currency and the base currency of the agreement. The exchange rate expresses how much it will cost in base currency to buy 100 units of the quote currency.

netAmount.description: The total quote amount in the quote currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total quote amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

grossAmount.description: The total quote amount in the quote currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

grossAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total quote amount in the base currency of the agreement after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the cost price of the items on the quote and the sales net quote amount in base currency.

marginPercentage.description: The margin expressed as a percentage. If the net quote amount is less than the cost price this number will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the quote in the quote currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the quote in base currency.

costPriceInBaseCurrency.description: The total cost of the items on the quote in the base currency of the agreement.

dueDate.description: The date the quote, is due for payment. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). This is only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate'.

attachment.description: A unique reference to the attachment file on the given draft quote.

layout.description: Layout to be applied for draft quotes and other documents for this customer.

layoutNumber.description: The unique identifier of the layout.

self.description: A unique link reference to the layout item.

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the quote.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the quote. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type duedate.

name.description: The name of the payment terms.

self.description: A unique reference to the payment term resource.

customer.description: The customer of the quote.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.

recipient.description: The actual recipient of the quote. This may be the same info found on the customer (and will probably be so in most cases) but it may also be a different recipient. For instance, the customer placing the quote may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the quote may be ACME IT.

name.description: The name of the actual recipient.

address.description: The street address of the actual recipient.

zip.description: The zip code of the actual recipient.

city.description: The city of the actual recipient.

country.description: The country of the actual recipient.

ean.description: The 'European Article Number' of the actual recipient.

publicEntryNumber.description: The public entry number of the actual recipient.

attention.description: The person to whom this quote is addressed.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer employee.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer employee.

vatZoneNumber.description: Unique identifier of the vat zone.

self.description: A unique reference to the vat zone.

cvr.description: The Corporate Identification Number of the recipient for example CVR in Denmark.

country.description: The country of the place of delivery

deliveryTerms.description: Details about the terms of delivery.

heading.description: The quote heading. Usually displayed at the top of the quote.

textLine1.description: The first line of supplementary text on the quote. This is usually displayed right under the heading in a smaller font.

textLine2.description: The second line of supplementary text in the notes on the quote. This is usually displayed as a footer on the quote.

references.description: Customer and company references related to this quote.

customerContact.description: The customer contact is a reference to the employee at the customer to contact regarding the quote.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer contact.

customer.description: The customer this contact belongs to.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.
A unique reference to the customer contact resource.

salesPerson.description: The sales person is a reference to the employee who sold the goods on the quote. This is also the person who is credited with this sale in reports.

self.description: A unique reference to the employee resource.

vendorReference.description: A reference to any second employee involved in the sale.

self.description: A unique reference to the employee resource.

other.description: A text field that can be used to save any custom reference on the quote.

project.description: The project the quote is connected to.

projectNumber.description: A unique identifier of the project.

self.description: A unique reference to the project resource.

lastUpdated.description: The last time the quote was updated. The date is formatted according to ISO-8601.

pdf.description: References to a pdf representation of this quote.

download.description: References a pdf representation of this quote.

quoteHandle.description: The unique reference of the SOAP quote handle.

id.description: ID of the SOAP quote handle.

templates.description: References to all templates on this sent quote.

upgradeInstructions.description: The self reference to the document to POST to /invoices/drafts to upgrade the quote to an invoice draft.

self.description: The self reference to the quote templates resource.
The unique self reference of the specific sent quote.

metaData.description: Information about possible actions, endpoints and resource paths related to the endpoint.

self.description: The unique self reference of the sent quote collection.

get.description: A schema for retrieving a sent quote.

200.description: A schema for retrieving a sent quote.

schema.description: A schema for retrieving a sent quote.

quoteNumber.description: A reference number for the quote document.

date.description: quote issue date. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

currency.description: The ISO 4217 currency code of the quote.

exchangeRate.description: The exchange rate between the quote currency and the base currency of the agreement. The exchange rate expresses how much it will cost in base currency to buy 100 units of the quote currency.

netAmount.description: The total quote amount in the quote currency before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

netAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total quote amount in the base currency of the agreement before all taxes and discounts have been applied.

grossAmount.description: The total quote amount in the quote currency after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

grossAmountInBaseCurrency.description: The total quote amount in the base currency of the agreement after all taxes and discounts have been applied.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the cost price of the items on the quote and the sales net quote amount in base currency.

marginPercentage.description: The margin expressed as a percentage. If the net quote amount is less than the cost price this number will be negative.

vatAmount.description: The total amount of VAT on the quote in the quote currency. This will have the same sign as net amount

roundingAmount.description: The total rounding error, if any, on the quote in base currency.

costPriceInBaseCurrency.description: The total cost of the items on the quote in the base currency of the agreement.

dueDate.description: The date the quote, is due for payment. Format according to ISO-8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). This is only used if the terms of payment is of type 'duedate'.

paymentTerms.description: The terms of payment for the quote.

paymentTermsNumber.description: A unique identifier of the payment term.

daysOfCredit.description: The number of days of credit on the quote. This field is only valid if terms of payment is not of type duedate.

name.description: The name of the payment terms.

self.description: A unique reference to the payment term resource.

customer.description: The customer of the quote.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.

recipient.description: The actual recipient of the quote. This may be the same info found on the customer (and will probably be so in most cases) but it may also be a different recipient. For instance, the customer placing the quote may be ACME Headquarters, but the recipient of the quote may be ACME IT.

name.description: The name of the actual recipient.

address.description: The street address of the actual recipient.

zip.description: The zip code of the actual recipient.

city.description: The city of the actual recipient.

country.description: The country of the actual recipient.

ean.description: The 'European Article Number' of the actual recipient.

publicEntryNumber.description: The public entry number of the actual recipient.

attention.description: The person to whom this quote is addressed.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer employee.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer employee.

vatZoneNumber.description: Unique identifier of the vat zone.

self.description: A unique reference to the vat zone.

cvr.description: The Corporate Identification Number of the recipient for example CVR in Denmark.

deliveryLocation.description: A reference to the place of delivery for the goods on the quote

deliveryLocationNumber.description: A unique identifier for the delivery location.

self.description: A unique reference to the delivery location resource.

delivery.description: The actual place of delivery for the goods on the quote. This is usually the same place as the one referenced in the deliveryLocation property, but may be edited as required.

address.description: Street address where the goods must be delivered to the customer.

zip.description: The zip code of the place of delivery.

city.description: The city of the place of delivery

country.description: The country of the place of delivery

deliveryTerms.description: Details about the terms of delivery.

heading.description: The quote heading. Usually displayed at the top of the quote.

textLine1.description: The first line of supplementary text on the quote. This is usually displayed right under the heading in a smaller font.

textLine2.description: The second line of supplementary text in the notes on the quote. This is usually displayed as a footer on the quote.

references.description: Customer and company references related to this quote.

customerContact.description: The customer contact is a reference to the employee at the customer to contact regarding the quote.

customerContactNumber.description: Unique identifier of the customer contact.

customer.description: The customer this contact belongs to.

customerNumber.description: The customer id number. The customer id number can be either positive or negative, but it can't be zero.

self.description: A unique reference to the customer resource.
A unique reference to the customer contact resource.

salesPerson.description: The sales person is a reference to the employee who sold the goods on the quote. This is also the person who is credited with this sale in reports.

self.description: A unique reference to the employee resource.

vendorReference.description: A reference to any second employee involved in the sale.

self.description: A unique reference to the employee resource.

other.description: A text field that can be used to save any custom reference on the quote.

project.description: The project the quote is connected to.

projectNumber.description: A unique identifier of the project.

self.description: A unique reference to the project resource.

lastUpdated.description: The last time the quote was updated. The date is formatted according to ISO-8601.

pdf.description: References a pdf representation of this quote.

download.description: The unique reference of the pdf representation for this quote.

quoteHandle.description: The unique reference of the SOAP quote handle.

id.description: ID of the SOAP quote handle.

templates.description: References to all templates on this sent quote.

upgradeInstructions.description: The self reference to the document to POST to /invoices/drafts to upgrade the quote to an invoice draft.

self.description: The self reference to the quote templates resource.

lines.description: An array containing the specific quote lines.

items.description: An array of the quote lines that make up the quote.

lineNumber.description: The line number is a unique number within the quote.

sortKey.description: A sort key used to sort the lines in ascending quote within the quote.

description.description: A description of the product or service sold.

accrual.description: Accrual is used to allocate costs and/or revenues over several periods.

startDate.description: The start date for the accrual. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

endDate.description: The end date for the accrual. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

unit.description: The unit of measure applied to the quote line.

unitNumber.description: The unique identifier of the unit.

name.description: The name of the unit (e.g. 'kg' for weight or 'l' for volume).

self.description: A unique reference to the unit resource.

product.description: The product or service offered on the quote line.

productNumber.description: The unique product number. This can be a stock keeping unit identifier (SKU).

self.description: A unique reference to the product resource.

quantity.description: The number of units of goods on the quote line.

unitNetPrice.description: The price of 1 unit of the goods or services on the quote line in the quote currency.

discountPercentage.description: A line discount expressed as a percentage.

unitCostPrice.description: The cost price of 1 unit of the goods or services in the quote currency.

marginInBaseCurrency.description: The difference between the net price and the cost price on the quote line in base currency.

marginPercentage.description: The margin on the quote line expressed as a percentage.

departmentalDistribution.description: A departmental distribution defines which departments this entry is distributed between. This requires the departments module to be enabled.

departmentalDistributionNumber.description: A unique identifier of the departmental distribution.

self.description: A unique reference to the departmental distribution resource.
The unique self reference of the sent quote.

get.description: A schema for retrieval of the self object.

200.description: A schema for retrieval of the self object.

schema.description: A schema for retrieval of the self object.

agreementNumber.description: The unique identifier of your e-conomic agreement.

agreementType.description: The type of agreement this is. It can either be denmark, sweden or norway

agreementTypeNumber.description: The unique identifier of your agreement type.

name.description: The name of the agreement type.

userName.description: The name of the the currently logged in user.

signupDate.description: The date the currently logged in user signed up. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

loginId.description: The unique identifier of the user.

email.description: The email of the user.

name.description: The name of the user.

agreementNumber.description: The unique identifier of the account of the currently logged user.

language.description: The currently logged in user's language.

languageNumber.description: The unique identifier of the language.

name.description: The name of the language.

self.description: A unique link reference to the language resource.

company.description: The currently logged in user's company.

zip.description: The company's postcode.

city.description: The company's city.

name.description: The company's name.

country.description: The company's country.

attention.description: The company's primary contact person.

email.description: The company's email.

companyIdentificationNumber.description: The company's company identification number. For example CVR in Denmark.

vatNumber.description: The company's value added tax identification number. This field is only available to agreements in Sweden. Not to be mistaken for the danish CVR number, which is defined on the 'companyIdentificationNumber' property.

bankSortCode.description: The registration number of the bank account.

bankAccountNumber.description: The account number in the company's bank.

pbsCustomerGroupNumber.description: Number used when registering an invoice to betalingsservice. Only used in Denmark.

pbsFiSupplierNumber.description: Number used when registering an invoice to betalingsservice. Only used in Denmark.

bankGiroNumber.description: The company's bankgiro number. Only used in Sweden and Norway.

appNumber.description: The unique identifier of the application.

name.description: The name of the application.

appPublicToken.description: The public token of the application used for other e-conomic customers to use the application.

created.description: The date the application was created. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

requiredRoles.description: The roles required to use this application.

items.description: An item in the roles array.

roleNumber.description: The unique identifier of the role.

name.description: The name of the role.

self.description: A unique link reference to the role resource.
A unique link reference to the application resource.

baseCurrency.description: The ISO 4217 code of the company's base currency.

defaultPaymentTerm.description: The default payment term for the customers of the company.

internationalLedger.description: If this value is 'true' then the international ledger is used.

companyAffiliation.description: The affiliation of the agreement. This can be 'E-conomic' or 'Developer'.

canSendElectronicInvoice.description: A boolean indicating if the company is setup to send electronic invoices. See http://wiki2.e-conomic.dk/salg/ean-faktura (Danish) for more information.

modules.description: The modules the company have active.

items.description: An item in the modules array.

moduleNumber.description: The unique identifier of the module.

name.description: The name of the module.

self.description: A unique link reference to the module resource.
